mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% persists in a zero lower bound regime, suppressing risk-free returns and forcing capital into equities, which produces positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day S&P 500 forward return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) during months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A declining UNRATE below 6% confirms accelerating labor market recovery, raises household income expectations and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 6% and falling.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad-based demand recovery that exceeds supply capacity, leading to positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The bivariate combination of RSAFS and HOUST growth produces a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, because accommodative financing costs amplify the earnings impact of higher industrial output.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 1%, rising INDPRO fails to produce positive mean 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in FEDFUNDS greater than 0.25 percentage points signals an unexpected monetary tightening that raises discount rates and reduces equity present values, generating negative S&P 500 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances following a >0.25pp monthly FEDFUNDS increase.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue over a quarterly horizon, driving positive S&P 500 60-day returns.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day S&P 500 forward return is not significantly positive during months when PAYEMS is increasing (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in HOUST exceeding 5% signals a durable rebound in construction and household formation, spurring cyclical equity outperformance and positive S&P 500 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns follow fewer than 55% of month-over-month >5% HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% generates negative real interest rates that erode fixed-income value and redirect capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 20-day returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
